steps.title: Contrast Anne and Marilla
steps.content: Anne is delighted by October colors and wants to decorate her room with maple branches. Marilla immediately calls the branches messy and says bedrooms are for sleeping. The scene is built around their opposite reactions.

steps.title: Choose the broader purpose
steps.content: Choice A focuses only on Anne, and Choice C focuses only on Marilla's disapproval. Choice B claims this is an argument they often have, which the excerpt does not establish. The point of the scene is the contrast between Anne's imaginative enthusiasm and Marilla's practicality. The answer is D.
